(2) Technical content review (logic, evidence, novelty, citations); (3) Writing and structure review (polish, organization).","rationale":{"R1.2":"Verifying logical consistency is load-bearing in a review to ensure the argument chain from motivation to discussion holds without flaws.","R1.3":"Aligning claims with reported evidence is central to validate whether results like residual values support the stated improvements.","R1.4":"Assessing novelty is load-bearing to estimate the delta of ResAdapt against prior works like Hassanpour & Mehta (1987).","R1.5":"Suggesting structural reorganization is a central expected responsibility to optimize the section-level narrative arc from setup to discussion.","R1.6":"Polishing writing is central for improving grammar, flow, and conciseness at the sentence and paragraph levels.","R1.7":"Ensuring citation accuracy and literature coverage is load-bearing for maintaining scholarly standards in the theoretical motivation.","RX.1":"Disclosing uncertainties is a central responsibility to identify any unmentioned limitations in the PINN training or residual analysis.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaims is load-bearing given the apparent mismatch between reported residuals and the interpretation of improvement.","RX.3":"Adhering to scope is central to confirm the section remains focused on ResAdapt for the 2D Burgers' equation without extraneous topics.","RX.4":"Avoiding downstream harms is peripheral but includes considering potential misapplications of adaptive PINN methods in critical simulations.","RX.5":"Ensuring provenance and traceability is central for verifying citations and the promised code release upon acceptance."}}
